mechanism_narrative: A falling 3-month trend in FEDFUNDS lowers the discount rate used for equity cash flows and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; rising FEDFUNDS reverses the sign of the signal.
regime_hint: monetary easing or zero-rate policy regime
failure_condition: Reject if months with declining 3-month FEDFUNDS changes do not produce higher average 20-day S&P 500 returns than months with rising FEDFUNDS, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses upward by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, labor-market deterioration signals falling earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the mean triggered return is not below the unconditional mean at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%, real short rates fall and nominal revenue expectations rise faster than discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger does not outperform periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.25% alone over 60 trading days, or if interaction-term coefficients are not positive in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in RSAFS and decline in UNRATE identifies a consumption-led labor recovery; stronger household income and spending drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises over 3 months and UNRATE falls over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-up and UNRATE-down condition has a 20-day win rate below 50% or fails to beat either single-series signal by at least 25 basis points per month.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO greater than 1% over 3 months signals weakening production volumes and operating leverage, reducing earnings expectations and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO contractions are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations or if average triggered returns are not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST rises more than 10% over 3 months from a level below 1200, improving residential construction demand signals cyclical recovery and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises > 10% over 3 months while HOUST < 1200
failure_condition: Reject if triggered housing rebounds do not produce positive 60-day returns in at least 55% of cases or if their mean return is not above the unconditional 60-day mean.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS expands while UNRATE remains above 5%, payroll recovery occurs with remaining labor slack, improving earnings expectations without immediate wage-pressure tightening and driving positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average gain > 100 thousand and UNRATE > 5%
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-growth and high-UNRATE interaction has a nonpositive coefficient or if triggered 60-day returns underperform unconditional returns in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDPC1 growth combined with rising RSAFS indicates broad demand expansion; stronger output and consumer spending raise expected corporate revenues and drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 latest quarterly growth > 0 and RSAFS rises over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have a 5-day S&P 500 win rate below 50% or if the interaction signal fails to improve out-of-sample accuracy versus GDPC1-only and RSAFS-only signals.
